[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2012-07-16
[TITLE]: Greenpeace activist dressed as polar bear arrested at petrol station protest | Metro News
[METADATA/INTRO]: This polar bear was one of many Greenpeace protesters targeting Shell petrol stations across the country to complain about the company’s plans to drill for oil off the coast of Alaska. This poor polar bear might be wishing it was...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: This polar bear was one of many Greenpeace protesters targeting Shell petrol stations across the country to complain about the company’s plans to drill for oil off the coast of Alaska. 
 This poor polar bear might be wishing it was back in the Arctic… after falling foul of the law in Britain. 
 OK, it looked guilty of making a silly gesture behind the back of an officer filming the crime scene. 
 And later its exasperated expression did seem to make a female officer laugh without due care and attention. 
 But as the crestfallen creature was handcuffed and dragged away to a waiting panda car by four officers, it looked in a rather bad way. But one thing’s for sure – the costumed campaigner had certainly made its point.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: A Greenpeace campaigner dressed as a polar bear is arrested during his attempt to shut down Shell petrol station in Edinburgh Stock Photo - Alamy
[METADATA/INTRO]: A Greenpeace campaigner dressed as a polar bear is arrested during his attempt to shut down Shell petrol station in Edinburgh Captions are provided by our contributors....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A Greenpeace campaigner dressed as a polar bear is arrested during his attempt to shut down Shell petrol station in Edinburgh 
 Captions are provided by our contributors. 
 60.2 MB (1.4 MB Compressed download)Releases: 
 Model - no | Property - noDo I need a release?Dimensions: 
 3744 x 5616 px | 31.7 x 47.5 cm | 12.5 x 18.7 inches | 300dpiDate taken: 
 Edinburgh , ScotlandMore information: 
 A Greenpeace campaigner dressed as a polar bear is arrested during his attempt to shut down Shell petrol station in Edinburgh just as the oil company is about to drill in the Arctic for the very first time. Greenpeace campaigners attempt to shut down every Shell petrol station in the capital cities of London and Edinburgh just as the oil company is about to drill in the Arctic for the very first time. The environmental group, who recently launched a star-studded campaign to save the Arctic, intend to close over 100 stations in London, and over a dozen in Edinburgh, by using the emergency shut-off switch, which stops petrol going to the pumps, and removing the fuse so it can’t immediately be turned back on again.Shell is due to begin drilling at two offshore sites in the Alaskan Arctic in the coming weeks, amid fears that an Arctic oil rush will be sparked and the push to carve up the region will accelerate. Russian oil giant Gazprom is also venturing into the Arctic this year
